---
title: "M. Fikru Rizal"
subtitle: "Research Fellow, Health Economics Group, Monash School of Public Health and Preventive Medicine"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.home-shell}
::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-copy}
<p class="eyebrow">Health economics | Development economics | Health policy</p>

# M. Fikru Rizal

Dr Muhammad Fikru Rizal is a Research Fellow in the Health Economics Group within the School of Public Health and Preventive Medicine at Monash University, Australia.

His research focuses on the socio-economic causes and consequences of health and healthcare service use, with particular interest in inequality, healthcare access and financing, mental health, and policy evaluation in Indonesia and other health-system settings.

::: {.hero-actions}
[Research](research.qmd){.primary-action}
[CV](cv.qmd){.secondary-action}
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=x1Dg-P8AAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao){.secondary-action}
:::
:::

::: {.hero-portrait}
![](assets/images/profile.jpg){fig-alt="Portrait of M. Fikru Rizal"}
:::
:::

::: {.signal-grid}
::: {.signal}
<span>Current role</span>
**Research Fellow**<br>
Monash University
:::

::: {.signal}
<span>Scholar snapshot</span>
**190 citations**<br>
h-index 5
:::

::: {.signal}
<span>Research areas</span>
**Health economics**<br>
mental health, development, policy
:::

::: {.signal}
<span>Policy work</span>
**Indonesia focus**<br>
financing, HTA, access
:::
:::

::: {.section-band}
## Profile

Fikru is a medical doctor by training from Indonesia. He holds an MSc in Health Economics from Erasmus University Rotterdam and a PhD in Health Economics from the Centre for Health Economics, Monash Business School. His doctoral research examined the economic determinants of health and healthcare utilisation in Indonesia.

He works with large longitudinal and linked datasets, applied econometric methods, inequality analysis, and health economic evaluation. Alongside academic research, he has worked with policy and consulting partners, primarily in Indonesia.
:::

::: {.section-band}
## Research Interests

::: {.research-grid}
::: {.research-card}
### Health economics
Healthcare use, economic evaluation, health-system performance, and policy design.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Inequality in health
Socioeconomic gradients in health outcomes, healthcare utilisation, and access.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Mental health
Treatment gaps, loneliness, social isolation, and quality-of-life measurement.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Development economics
Applied research on health and welfare in low- and middle-income country settings.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.cta-band}
For publications, working papers, and projects, see [Research](research.qmd). For education, appointments, teaching, and consulting experience, see [CV](cv.qmd).
:::
:::
